seoTitle: Import Mode vs DirectQuery in Power BI: Which to Pick
metaDescription: Compare Import Mode and DirectQuery in Power BI so you can choose the right data connectivity option for performance, freshness, and dataset size.
intro: Power BI gives you two core ways to connect to data, Import Mode and DirectQuery, and picking the wrong one can hurt either performance or data freshness. This guide breaks down how each mode works and when to use it, so you can match your connection method to your actual reporting needs. It's aimed at anyone setting up a new report or troubleshooting a slow one.

steps.heading: Understand how Import Mode works
steps.body: Import Mode loads a copy of your data into Power BI's own engine, which generally delivers fast performance but relies on scheduled refreshes to stay current.

steps.heading: Understand how DirectQuery works
steps.body: DirectQuery leaves the data in its source system and queries it live, which keeps information up to date but depends on the source database's performance.

steps.heading: Compare performance considerations
steps.body: Weigh Import Mode's typically faster report interactions against DirectQuery's ability to reflect real-time or near real-time data changes.

steps.heading: Match the mode to your business scenario
steps.body: Choose Import Mode for large, less time-sensitive datasets and DirectQuery for scenarios where users need to see current data, such as live operational dashboards.

steps.heading: Set up the connection in Power BI
steps.body: Select your preferred mode when connecting to a data source, keeping in mind that this choice affects how the rest of your report behaves.

faq.question: Which mode is faster, Import Mode or DirectQuery?
faq.answer: Import Mode is generally faster for report interactions because data is stored locally, while DirectQuery speed depends on the performance of the source database.

faq.question: Can I switch between Import Mode and DirectQuery later?
faq.answer: Switching is possible but can require rebuilding parts of your data model, so it's worth choosing carefully at the start of a project.

faq.question: Does DirectQuery show real-time data?
faq.answer: It queries the source live each time, which is close to real time, though actual freshness still depends on how current the underlying source data is.

faq.question: Is there a dataset size limit with Import Mode?
faq.answer: Import Mode has practical size limits based on your Power BI license and available memory, which is one reason very large datasets sometimes favor DirectQuery.

faq.question: Can I combine Import Mode and DirectQuery in one report?
faq.answer: Yes, Power BI supports composite models that mix both modes, though this adds complexity to how the data model behaves.
